title: The Brightest Radio Flash Ever Found
summary: Astronomers have found the brightest fast radio burst ever seen and discovered where it came from. Fast radio bursts are powerful flashes of radio energy from space that last only a short time. This new burst, called RBFLOAT, was detected on March 16, 2025, and lasted about one-fifth of a second. Using a network of telescopes called the CHIME/FRB Outrigger array, scientists pinpointed its origin to a galaxy named NGC 4141, which is about 130 million light-years away in the constellation Ursa Major. This galaxy is relatively close to Earth, making the burst appear very bright. The team also used the James Webb Space Telescope to look at the same spot and found a faint infrared signal, which might come from a star or be related to the burst. This discovery helps scientists learn more about these mysterious space events.

background_read: Fast radio bursts are mysterious flashes of energy from space that scientists are trying to understand. They happen very quickly and come from far away. Astronomers use telescopes to study them and learn about the universe. This article talks about a special burst that was very bright and close, helping scientists get more clues.
Article_Structure: WHO: Astronomers from around the world, including researchers from the University of Toronto.
WHAT: They found the brightest fast radio burst ever, named RBFLOAT, and traced it to a galaxy.
WHERE: The burst came from galaxy NGC 4141 in the constellation Ursa Major, about 130 million light-years from Earth.
WHY: To learn more about fast radio bursts and where they come from in space.

perspectives.description: This event helps scientists understand mysterious space phenomena better.
